mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing present values of future cash flows and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Rank IC for FEDFUNDS changes remains below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows, or forward 20-day returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.10) when rates decline.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.4 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending expectations and corporate earnings forecasts, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE rises > 0.4pp, or the signal out-of-sample accuracy drops below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates broad-based domestic demand recovery, boosting corporate revenue growth and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The joint positive signal yields negative 60-day forward returns in > 60% of occurrences, or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.05) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during a late-cycle expansion phase reflects capacity utilization nearing limits, prompting expectations of supply bottlenecks and margin compression that produce negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration yields positive 5-day returns in > 65% of late-cycle observations, or the mechanism fails to separate from baseline returns (t-stat < 1.5) in regime-filtered samples.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls increases aggregate wage income and consumer purchasing power, supporting corporate earnings growth and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Rolling 3-year Rank IC for PAYEMS growth remains below 0.01, or 20-day forward returns are uncorrelated with payroll changes (Spearman rho < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly CPIAUCSL increase exceeding 0.4% triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, raising discount rates and compressing equity valuation multiples, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns remain positive in > 60% of high-inflation trigger months, or the threshold model predictive accuracy falls below 45% in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between quarterly GDP growth and monthly retail sales indicates weakening consumer demand relative to overall economic output, signaling earnings headwinds that drive negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The divergence signal produces positive 60-day returns in > 55% of cases, or the interaction effect loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) across multiple rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment alongside stagnant payroll growth during a post-shock recovery phase indicates structural labor market weakness that suppresses consumer confidence and corporate investment, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 20-day returns in > 60% of triggered regime instances, or the joint labor signal shows no incremental predictive power over single-series baselines (F-test p > 0.05).
